text: Hi guys! So today we have a really special episode. I'm joining some other

She is an amazing mom and has impeccable taste. But best of all she's having a
baby in November and what would a baby shower be without a diaper cake? So today
I'm gonna show you guys a modern twist on this classic tradition. I'm Brooke and
I'm passionate about making beautiful things for my family on a budget. And
here's what we're gonna need to get started. 75 to 100 plain white diapers,
some decorative ribbon, rubber bands and safety pins, a 14 inch cake circle, three
different size round baking pans, an empty or almost empty paper towel roll,
and some kind of cake topper. I've chosen these decorative animals. First we're
gonna build the base of the cake so you're gonna need your largest pan. I've
chosen a 12 inch, a 9 inch, and a 6 inch and I'm gonna
start with a stack of about 10 diapers and just fan them out around the
perimeter of the pan. And you're gonna want to make sure you have the butt
pointing out and the top flat tucked in. I'm overlapping them at about two inch
intervals. When you get to the end you're gonna tuck the top of the first two
diapers behind the bottom of the last two so that you have one fluid circle.
This is what it should look like and then we're just gonna take the rest of
our diapers and use the same motion around the paper towels and just fan
them out and fill it in.
So the first thing you're gonna do is take your paper towels and fill them in.
So this is what it should look like. So don't worry if it's a little bit too long.
You can take the rest of your diapers and just fill them in.
So then you're gonna take the rest of your diapers and just fill them in.
So the base is done and now we need to secure it with a rubber band.
Yay!
So our first tier is finished and we're going to take it out of the pan and then go ahead
and do the same thing for our two smaller tiers.
So the body of the cake is done and we're just going to turn this upside down and insert
it in the middle and this will hold everything together.
Now for the fun part, we get to decorate our cake.
I'm going to take this burlap ribbon, you can use anything, but we just want to cover
the outside edges so that we hide that rubber band.
I love this burlap ribbon because it just gives it a soft organic feel.
I'm going to add a layer of lace over the burlap just to make it a little more feminine
since Joy's having a girl.
And then I'm going to pin both layers of ribbon together with these cute little cloth diaper
pins.
